seoTitle: Sunnyside vs Sunset — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sunnyside and Sunset. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunnyside and Sunset.
heading: Sunnyside vs Sunset — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunnyside, Queensland and Sunset, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sunnyside has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Sunset (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunnyside or Sunset safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunnyside has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Sunset scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunnyside scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Sunset scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunnyside or Sunset?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sunnyside has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
